title: Iran Sugar — Consumption, Imports by Country, and State Monopoly Production, 1890-2002 (Encyclopaedia Iranica)
source_org: Encyclopaedia Iranica (article 'SUGAR' by Willem M. Floor), ultimately citing British consular Diplomatic and Consular Reports (DCR), the Dutch legation in Tehran, and the International Sugar Organization
retrieved_by: claude (Claude Browser tool for narrative text + curl for table image)
frequency: annual, sparse except Table 1 (1906/07-1913/14, near-continuous with a 1909-11 gap in the original source)
units: mixed: tons, GBP, qerans, percent, days, tons/day, workers, factories, kg/person/year -- see unit column per row
license_terms: Encyclopaedia Iranica content copyrighted, used here for non-commercial research/citation with full attribution.
notes: This is the richest single source found in this hunt: a genuine numeric table (TABLE 1, 'Value of Loaf Sugar Imported into Persia from 1906-07 to 1913-14', a 9-country x 6-year breakdown in GBP) PLUS ~30 additional narrative-embedded numeric data points spanning 1890-2002, covering Qajar-era import competition (Russia vs. France vs. Belgium etc.), the 1932 founding of the modern state sugar-refining monopoly (8 factories, 4,200 workers, 34,000 tons output), postwar consumption/import data, 1970s-1980s consumption growth (650,000 tons in 1970 to 1,100,000 tons in 1980), and year-2000 production/trade figures (Iran ranked 7th largest global beet-sugar producer per the International Sugar Organization). TABLE 1 was VISUALLY VERIFIED by downloading the source's own table image (sugar_table_1.jpg, confirmed present via DOM inspection -- unlike the CARPETS articles, which had zero embedded table images) and reading it directly with the Read tool; no OCR was used. One arithmetic discrepancy is flagged in the 1906-07 row (country breakdown sums to ~0.3% more than the printed total) and transcribed as-printed without correction. One year was corrected from an evident single-digit typo in the source ('1656-57' -> 1956, explained in that row's notes) since 1656 is chronologically impossible for a topic that starts in the 1850s. This complements FAOSTAT (which independently covers Iran sugar-beet/cane physical production and trade quantity+value from 1961 onward -- confirmed via grep of data/raw/faostat/tcl-trade-crops-livestock/filtered_iran_and_comparators.csv) by covering: (a) the entire pre-1961 period FAOSTAT cannot reach, and (b) the fiscal/monopoly-structure dimension (workforce, factory count, state-monopoly status, import value BY COUNTRY OF ORIGIN) that FAOSTAT, as a pure production/trade-quantity database, never captures at any date.
failures: Article references TABLE 2, TABLE 3, and TABLE 4 (import/export commodity shares, industry growth under the state monopoly, and 1997 UN sugar trade balance respectively) but only TABLE 1's image is hosted on the live site (confirmed: sugar_table_2.jpg through sugar_table_4.jpg all return HTTP 404). These three tables' data could not be recovered.
